example.en: My sister and I are very close.
example.vi: Chị em tôi rất thân thiết.
example.es: Mi hermana y yo somos muy unidas.

definitions.en: Sister; a term used to refer to a female sibling.
definitions.vi: Chị em; thuật ngữ dùng để chỉ một người chị hoặc em gái.
definitions.es: Hermana; un término utilizado para referirse a una hermana femenina.

exampleSentences: Chị em thường cùng nhau đi mua sắm vào cuối tuần.
Mỗi khi có chuyện buồn, chị em luôn chia sẻ với nhau.
Gia đình anh có bốn chị em rất thân thiết và yêu thương nhau.

translation.en: I go out with my sister on the weekend.

content.sentence: Tôi đi chơi với ___ vào cuối tuần.

notes:
Chị em means sisters — usually biological sisters in the same family.  

It’s a plural noun phrase. You can count it with numbers in front: hai chị em, ba chị em (no extra classifier needed). Often used to talk about the relationship as a unit, not each person separately.  

It can also mean female siblings in general (including cousins in some contexts) or even women who are close like sisters.  

Memory tip: chị = older sister, so chị em often focuses on the sister side of siblings.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Sisters often go shopping together on weekends.
Whenever there is something sad, sisters always share with each other.
His family has four sisters who are very close and love each other.
notes_md:
**Chị em** means *sisters* — usually biological sisters in the same family.  

It’s a plural noun phrase. You can count it with numbers in front: *hai chị em, ba chị em* (no extra classifier needed). Often used to talk about the relationship as a unit, not each person separately.  

It can also mean *female siblings in general* (including cousins in some contexts) or even *women who are close like sisters*.  

Memory tip: **chị** = older sister, so *chị em* often focuses on the sister side of siblings.

content.sentence: Tôi đi chơi với ___ vào cuối tuần.

translation.en: I go out with my sister on the weekend.
